question: Does the image depict only one of the two conflicting species (either Polar Bear or Penguins), effectively resolving the ecological error?
Does the map inset highlight the correct geographic region corresponding to the remaining animal (Arctic circle for Polar Bear, Antarctica for Penguins)?
Is the central text box narrative corrected to remove the statement that the two species share the same habitat?
Are all remaining text elements (Location, Species names, 'Arctic/Antarctic' labels, subtitles) fully updated to be factually consistent with the single remaining species?
Is the area where the removed animal(s) stood seamlessly inpainted with snow/ice textures that match the surrounding lighting and perspective?
Are the footprints on the snow consistent with the remaining animal(s) (i.e., tracks belonging to the removed species are erased)?
Is the edited image free of visible compositing errors, unnatural edge artifacts, or blurriness?
Is all text (both preserved and modified) crisp, legible, and free of rendering artifacts?
